memes.label: "To the Reader"
memes.description: "This is a first book, and in it the author has written of experience which is now far and lost, but which was once part of the fabric of his life."

memes.label: Altamont High School & Leonard's Fitting School
"Chapter 1"
memes.description: "A destiny that leads the English to the Dutch is strange enough; but one that leads from Epsom into Pennsylvania, and thence into the hills that shut in Altamon."

memes.label: "Chapter 2"
memes.description: "Oliver married Eliza in May. After their wedding trip to Philadelphia, they returned to the house he had built for her on Woodson Street."

memes.label: "Chapter 3"
memes.description: "In the great processional of the years through which the history of the Gants was evolving, few years had borne a heavier weight of pain, terror, and wretchedne."

memes.label: "Chapter 4"
memes.description: "The baby was, to reverse an epigram, an unconscionable time in getting born; but when Gant finally awoke just after ten o’clock next morning, whimpering from ta."

memes.label: "Chapter 5"
memes.description: "Luke got well after cursing doctor, nurse, and family for several weeks: it was stubborn typhoid."

memes.label: "Chapter 6"
memes.description: "The death of Grover gave Eliza the most terrible wound of her life: her courage was snapped, her slow but powerful adventure toward freedom was abruptly stopped."

memes.label: "Chapter 7"
memes.description: "This journey to California was Gant’s last great voyage. He made it two years after Eliza’s return from St. Louis, when he was fifty-six years old."

memes.label: "Chapter 8"
memes.description: "Eugene was loose now in the limitless meadows of sensation: his sensory equipment was so complete that at the moment of perception of a single thing, the whole."

memes.label: "Chapter 9"
memes.description: "Yes, and in that month when Prosperpine comes back, and Ceres’ dead heart rekindles, when all the woods are a tender smoky blur, and birds no bigger than a budd."

memes.label: "Chapter 10"
memes.description: "But this freedom, this isolation in print, this dreaming and unlimited time of fantasy, was not to last unbroken."

memes.label: "Chapter 11"
memes.description: "Eliza saw Altamont not as so many hills, buildings, people: she saw it in the pattern of a gigantic blueprint."

memes.label: "Chapter 12"
memes.description: "It was the winter, and the sullen dying autumn that he hated most at Dixieland—the dim fly-specked lights, the wretched progress about the house in search of wa."

memes.label: "Chapter 13"
memes.description: "In the years that followed, up to his eleventh or twelfth year when he could no longer travel on half fares, Eugene voyaged year by year into the rich mysteriou."

memes.label: "Chapter 14"
memes.description: "The plum tree, black and brittle, rocks stiffly in winter wind. Her million little twigs are frozen in spears of ice."

memes.label: "Chapter 15"
memes.description: "The mountains were his masters. They rimmed in life. They were the cup of reality, beyond growth, beyond struggle and death."

memes.label: "Chapter 16"
memes.description: "The Spring grew ripe. There was at midday a soft drowsiness in the sun."

memes.label: "Chapter 17"
memes.description: "Eugene spent the next four years of his life in Leonard’s school."

memes.label: "Chapter 18"
memes.description: "In the years that had followed Eliza’s removal to Dixieland, by a slow inexorable chemistry of union and repellence, profound changes had occurred in the alignm."

memes.label: "Chapter 19"
memes.description: "One afternoon in the young summer, Gant leaned upon the rail, talking to Jannadeau."

memes.label: "Chapter 20"
memes.description: "Gant, during these years in which Helen and Luke, the two for whom he felt the deepest affection, were absent a large part of the time, lived a splintered exist."

memes.label: "Chapter 21"
memes.description: "During the first years of this illness Gant showed a diminished, but not a seriously impaired, energy."

memes.label: "Chapter 22"
memes.description: "Toward the beginning of Eugene’s fourteenth year, when he had been a student at Leonard’s for two years, Ben got work for him as a paper carrier."

memes.label: "Chapter 23"
memes.description: "He did not tell the Leonards that he was working in the early morning."

memes.label: "Chapter 24"
memes.description: "With thick chalked fingers John Dorsey thoughtfully massaged his torso from loin to chin."

memes.label: "Chapter 25"
memes.description: "Yes. The enormous crime had been committed. And, for almost a year, Eugene had been maintaining a desperate neutrality. His heart, however, was not neutral."

memes.label: "Chapter 26"
memes.description: "In the autumn, at the beginning of his fifteenth year—his last year at Leonard’s—Eugene went to Charleston on a short excursion."

memes.label: "Chapter 27"
memes.description: "My Shakespeare, rise! He rose. The bard rose throughout the length and breadth of his brave new world. He was not for an age, but for all time."

memes.label: "Chapter 28"
memes.description: "Eugene was not quite sixteen years old when he was sent away to the university."

memes.label: "Chapter 29"
memes.description: "Before his first year was ended, the boy had changed his lodging four or five times."

memes.label: "Chapter 30"
memes.description: "There was at Dixieland a girl named Laura James. She was twenty-one years old. She looked younger. She was there when he came back."

memes.label: "Chapter 31"
memes.description: "One day, when June was coming to its end, Laura James said to him: “I shall have to go home next week."

memes.label: "Chapter 32"
memes.description: "When he returned to the university for his second year, he found the place adjusted soberly to war."

memes.label: "Chapter 33"
memes.description: "During the few remaining days of his holiday, he stayed almost entirely away from the house, coming for a brief and mumbled meal, and late at night, for bed."

memes.label: "Chapter 34"
memes.description: "Eugene returned to Altamont two weeks before the term began at Pulpit Hill. The town and the nation seethed in the yeasty ferment of war."

memes.label: "Chapter 35"
memes.description: "There was no train until the next day."

memes.label: "Chapter 36"
memes.description: "In that enormous silence, where pain and darkness met, some birds were waking. It was October. It was almost four o’clock in the morning."

memes.label: "Chapter 37"
memes.description: "So, to Ben dead was given more care, more time, more money than had ever been given to Ben living."

memes.label: "Chapter 38"
memes.description: "Three weeks after Eugene’s return to the university the war ended. The students cursed and took off their uniforms."

memes.label: "Chapter 39"
memes.description: "Gant and Eliza came to his graduation. He found them lodgings in the town: it was early June—hot, green, fiercely and voluptuously Southern."

memes.label: "Chapter 40"
memes.description: "The Square lay under blazing moonlight. The fountain pulsed with a steady breezeless jet: the water fell upon the pool with a punctual slap."

memes.label: Guy Doak
memes.description: A sharp, cynical Yankee student from Newark, New Jersey, who rooms with Eugene at the Leonards' school — his crisp, dogmatic rationalism provides a foil to the florid Southern romanticism of Eugene's other classmates.

memes.label: Miss Florry Mangle
memes.description: A trained nurse at Dixieland — limp-faced, whining, and morbid — who courts with Jake Clapp and attends the dying W.O. Gant with a silence that gives "morbid comfort."

memes.label: Judge Webster Tayloe
memes.description: A retired corporation counsel of eminent prosperity and aristocratic contempt — his walnut-paneled bedchamber in Lunn's Cove and his rich acres below Rheinhart's mark the social summit of the Altamont region.

memes.label: Miss Amy
memes.description: A teacher at Margaret Leonard's school — large, warm, gypsy-eyed, and full of a lazy affectionate contempt for Eugene Gant's brilliant laziness.

memes.label: Miss Groody
memes.description: Eugene Gant's elementary school teacher — a gaunt, red-faced spinster with fierce glaring eyes — who becomes the target of obscene rhymes composed by Eugene and Otto in their schoolboy mischief.

memes.label: Miss Newton
memes.description: A "wrenny and neurotic old maid, with asthma" who becomes Eliza Gant's unofficial assistant in managing Dixieland — one of the permanent residents who gives the boarding house its atmosphere of decayed permanence.

memes.label: Mr. Torrington
memes.description: A Rhodes Scholar and English teacher at Pulpit Hill — lisping, moistly smiling, and gently contemptuous of Eugene Gant's taste for "flashy" writers who are "not in line with the best modern thought."

memes.label: Mr. Willis
memes.description: A student in Eugene's class at the Altamont school who offers a memorably inadequate definition of a stick — "a piece of wood" — in a brief but vivid classroom exchange.

memes.label: Mrs. Barton
memes.description: Hugh Barton's massive, kindly mother — a woman of slow emphatic approval whose near-fatal illness on Helen's wedding night becomes the first trial of the young marriage.

memes.label: Mrs. Bowden
memes.description: A small, worn dressmaker and widow who chaperones Eugene Gant's trip to Charleston — a figure of quiet endurance whose modest insurance-money vacation becomes the backdrop for Eugene's sexual awakening.

memes.label: Mrs. Bradley
memes.description: A flabby, petulant widow who runs a lodging-house near the university — Eugene pays her two months in advance and finds her food excellent but her son insufferable.

memes.label: Mrs. Clarence Firkins
memes.description: A hostess of Altamont's "Younger Set" whose dinner dance at Snotwood is chronicled in Wolfe's satirical society column — a figure of aspiration and absurdity in the town's social pageantry.

memes.label: Mrs. Selborne
memes.description: A tall, heavy-bodied blonde boarder at Dixieland — a sensuous South Carolinian whose rich laughter and air of tender allurement make her a figure of fascination and desire for the Gant men.

memes.label: Bob Sterling
memes.description: Eugene Gant's college roommate at the start of his second year — the best student at Altamont High School, a neat and earnest young man with a fatal heart condition that claims his life within weeks.

memes.label: Dick Ravenel
memes.description: A literary-minded young surgeon and colleague of Hugh McGuire, noted for his surgical skill, dry wit, and the restrained affection that "grew behind a wall."

memes.label: Jennings Ware
memes.description: A robust seventeen-year-old country boy who trains Eugene Gant on his first paper route before leaving for better employment at the newspaper office.

memes.label: Ralph Rolls
memes.description: A schoolmate of Eugene Gant, part of the group of young men who perform in the Shakespeare pageant — characterized by his derby hat, pert freckled face, tobacco-chewing, and coarse humor.

memes.label: Roy Duncan
memes.description: A college student who serves as Tom French's "court jester" — a hanger-on with a small freckled face and a characteristic "high hard cackle" who participates in the mockery of Eugene Gant.

memes.label: Tyson Smathers
memes.description: A young newsboy in Altamont who delivers papers on Eugene Gant's old route — steady, freckled, and Scotch-Irish — in a brief but resonant dawn encounter that evokes Eugene's own boyhood.

memes.label: Mrs. Franz Wilhelm Von Zeck
memes.description: The wealthy patroness of the arts in Altamont, wife of Dr. Franz Wilhelm Von Zeck — a Wagnerian grande dame who sponsors musical education and embodies the town's aspirational high culture.

memes.label: World War I & The American Army
